data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0010/sessions/0023-horizon-T12

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
Confirmed — the round is complete and stable:

- **Working tree clean** across my write scope (project, blueprints, roadmap).
- **My two commits are in the ledger** (`a756ff8` blueprint `\lean`-name/header fix, `a18a541` roadmap 01XJ advance); the tex fix is present, and the orchestrator has since published dashboards (`c66de2b`, `9b001ab`) — confirming it owns canonical cache regeneration, which validates reverting my format-divergent cache churn.
- **Inbox tidy**: 1 open info (`I-0091`, the reconcile), 1 open issue (`I-0087`, the valid union-merge blueprint gap), memory pruned of the two superseded items.

Nothing is pending in scope. Task complete: run 0010 T12 r1 is reviewed and reconciled — the Stacks 01XJ closure (`pushforward_isQuasicoherent`) is verified green and axiom-clean, the one blueprint mismatch it exposed is fixed and committed, the roadmap advance is logged, and orientation (`recommendation.md`) is left for the next agent. The only carried-forward items are the orchestrator's cache refresh, the `I-0087` blueprint pass, and a future janitor memory-cap prune — all noted in `I-0091` and the report above.

# Task focus
Task: T12
Title: Picard scheme representability (FGA) — Pic_{C/k} representable, locally of finite type
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean
References: roadmap=AJC.picrep

Prove **representability of the relative Picard functor by a scheme** (FGA / Grothendieck FGA 232 / Theorem 4.8 in Kleiman) — roadmap cone `AJC.picrep`. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with a $k$-rational point, the relative Picard functor is representable by a $k$-scheme locally of finite type:

$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable by a scheme, locally of finite type}.$$

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- **FBC member-node patterns (session 0007-T2) — the essImage route that closed `IsIso bareBC`.**

- **Specialize the Beck–Chevalley square to `p = V₀.ι` (`V₀ : X.Opens`, `IsAffineOpen V₀`)** instead of an abstract affine open immersion `p : V ⟶ X`. Every consumer instantiates that form (`coverInterOpen`), and it makes qcoh bookkeeping FREE via iterated `isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens` — no general 'pullback along open immersion preserves qcoh' needed (that exists only in sorry-tainted `QuotScheme.lean`, do NOT import it).
- **Prove 'pullback of pushforward is a pushforward' as essImage membership, with the ring square the ABSTRACT pushout** `B := pushout φ ψ` (`IsPushout.of_hasPushout`): geometric cartesianness is free (`isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout`), `Spec σ` open-immersion by stability, its range via `IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackSnd` + `hpb.isoPullback_hom_snd` + `Set.range_eq_univ.mpr h.surjective`, and the final match with the preimage open by `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq` + `pushforwardComp/Congr`. NO four-corner isoSpec transport of a concrete square.
- **`pullback e.hom ≅ pushforward e.inv`** (scheme iso e): `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e.hom) (pushforwardEquivOfIso e).symm.toAdjunction` — one-liner, no defeq wall.
- **Open-open pushforward–restriction commutation**: the `glueOverlapBaseChangeIso` 4-step pattern (`SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso(eqToIso) ≪≫ pushforwardCongr ≪≫ pushforwardComp.symm`) transfers verbatim to the square `opensInterLift V₀ U ≫ V₀.ι = (U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V₀).ι ≫ U.ι`; the appLE-coherence proof is the same 5 lines.
- **Pointwise scheme-morphism reasoning**: derive morphism-level equalities first (cancel isos with `cancel_mono`), then apply at points with `Scheme.Hom.comp_apply`; raw `ConcreteCategory.hom`-coercion mismatches from `Set.mem_image` unfold are defeq — bridge with `have h' : U.ι y = V₀.ι x := hyx` + `show`.
- **Names**: `Opens.grothendieckTopology` (root ns), `Scheme.Hom.comp_apply`, `Scheme.Hom.comp_preimage`, `IsAffineOpen.inf` (needs `[IsAffineHom (pullback.diagonal (terminal.from X))]` ⟸ `IsSeparated.isClosedImmersion_diagonal`), `Scheme.Hom.isAffineOpen_iff_of_isOpenImmersion`, `IsOpenImmersion.of_comp`.
- The whole 430-LOC file (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`) compiled with ~3 error-fix iterations — the iso-chain compositions (`pushforwardComp/Congr/Id .app` chains) hit NO v4.31 transparency walls.
- **leandag audit convention: the DAG engine registers only STATEMENT-level `\uses`; proof-level `\uses` are ignored.**

- Consequence: a blueprint node whose only dependencies are declared inside its proof block shows as **isolated** in `horizon leandag`, even though the math dependency is real and the proof is complete. This is expected, not a blueprint defect.
- Observed run 0006 (Albanese): 297 nodes, **178 isolated**, but **0 dangling** and 287 proved — the high isolated count is entirely this artifact.
- **The health metric to trust is `dangling` (should be 0) and `proved`, NOT `isolated`.** Do not chase isolated nodes as missing edges; if you want them linked in the DAG, lift the relevant `\uses` from the proof block up to the statement line.
- Same engine limitation was noted by the T6 Horizon session; recorded here so future Ground audits don't misread it.
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- Recipe: pole-divisor purity WITHOUT normality/UFD of regular local rings (Albanese `PolePurity.lean`, axiom-clean) — reuse for any "unmixedness of principal ideals" need.

Mathlib v4.31 has NO regular⟹UFD (Auslander–Buchsbaum), NO Serre normality criterion, NO Krull-domain $R=\bigcap_{\mathrm{ht}=1}R_\mathfrak p$; `RingTheory/RegularLocalRing/` is only `Defs.lean`. Do not build those. The elementary substitute chain:

- **Swap lemma** (`mem_span_singleton_of_swap_pair`): domain, $u\ne 0$, $v$ regular mod $(u)$, $ux, vx \in (b)$ ⟹ $x \in (b)$ via $b(ut-vs)=0$. Replaces the determinant trick / integral-closedness entirely.
- **Regular pair in regular local of dim ≥ 2** (`IsRegularLocalRing.exists_swap_pair_of_two_le_ringKrullDim`): $u \in \mathfrak m\setminus\mathfrak m^2$ + project lemmas `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` and `isDomain_of_regularLocal` (00NP, AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean — CONFIRMED axiom-clean); pick $v$ lifting a nonzero element of the quotient's maximal ideal.
- **Ass-prime height bound** (`Ideal.height_le_one_of_colon_span_singleton`): prime $Q=((b):y)$ with $A_Q$ regular ⟹ $\mathrm{ht}\,Q\le 1$; the colon persists to $\mathfrak m_{A_Q}$ (map ≤ colon via `Localization.AtPrime.map_eq_maximalIdeal`, colon proper ⟹ ≤ maximalIdeal).
- **Main thm over $R_\mathfrak p$** so the associated prime lands below $\mathfrak p$ (`exists_le_isAssociatedPrime_of_isNoetherianRing` + `isAssociatedPrime_iff`); transfer with the golden Mathlib trio: `IsLocalization.height_under` (heights under comap of localization), `IsLocalization.localizationLocalizationAtPrimeIsoLocalization` (double localization ≃ₐ), `IsRegularLocalRing.of_ringEquiv`.
- **Scheme wrapper traps**: state membership as `algebraMap (X.presheaf.stalk z) X.functionField).range` (instances `stalkFunctionFieldAlgebra`, `functionField_isScalarTower`, `functionField_isFractionRing_of_isAffineOpen` are all ready-made for integral X); point-from-prime via `hU.fromSpec` + `hU.isLocalization_stalk'` (takes the PrimeSpectrum point directly — no primeIdealOf round-trip); `Order.coheight z = 1` via CoheightBridge `ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` + `IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`. Hypotheses of form `∀ q, IsRegularLocalRing (Localization.AtPrime q)` MUST use instance binder `∀ (q : Ideal R) [q.IsPrime], …` (plain `→` fails elaboration). `Submodule.mem_colon_singleton` gives `r • y ∈ …` — always explicit `rw [smul_eq_mul]`, never `simpa [smul_eq_mul] using` (fails). `IsLocalization.mk'_surjective` returns a beta-unreduced match — fix with `replace hcs : IsLocalization.mk' … = g := hcs`. Quotient-module annihilator bridge: `Algebra.smul_def` + `Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq` (NOT `map_smul`/`Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul`).

Remaining Milne 3.3 gaps after this: substeps 1–3 (difference rational map + slicing + $\Phi^*\mathcal O_{G,e}$; needs function-field pullback for `Scheme.RationalMap`) and 4b (diagonal codim bound: `ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_add_card` in Mathlib `KrullDimension/Regular.lean` is the Krull tool; diagonal ideal $n$-generation from smoothness still to build). For non-closed diagonal points, the slicing in substep 2 works at the GENERIC point of the fiber $\mathrm{pr}_1^{-1}(x) = \operatorname{Spec}\kappa(x)\times_{\bar k}X$ (irreducible by geometric irreducibility; its generic point maps to $\eta_X \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)$ under $\mathrm{pr}_2$) — this resolves the "Milne argues with closed points" worry.
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.
- `instPicSharpRepresentable` proved by `choose_spec`; `groupSchemeStructure : CommGrpObj (PicScheme C)` proved via `CommGrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + a `Multiplicative.ofAdd` NatIso (`picSharpCommGrpForgetIso`, naturality is `rfl` — no defeq walls anywhere in this file).

For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.

- `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` (new, ~185 LOC, axiom-clean): `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (g : Y ⟶ X)` for ARBITRARY scheme morphisms. Recipe: localize the pullback, not the presentation — per cover member `A`, `(g^*F)|_{g⁻¹A} ≅ (g.resLE A W)^*(F|_A)` via `pullbackComp`+`pullbackCongr (resLE_comp_ι g le_rfl)`+`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, transport the global presentation of `F|_A` by `SheafOfModules.Presentation.map` (15-underscore @-application, copy `presentationRestrictSliceOfOver`'s shape), then the CechTermAcyclic over-bridge tail (`modulesOverOpensEquivalence` / `overOpensIsoRestrict` / `overOpensFunctorUnitIso` / counit) and `IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop` on the preimage cover.

FBC leaves 5 → 4.

- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.

Chevalley–Rosenlicht).

At session start check `pgrep -af claude` + parent PIDs; terminate a same-project claude whose horizon runner is dead, but read its transcript tail first and inherit its pending intent (session 0011's uncommitted surgery was sound and became the session's main result).

- Recipe: building ModuleCat-R morphisms into sheaf-of-modules section objects (v4.31) — the ConcreteCategory.ofHom + presheaf-obj pattern, and the let-fvar IsIso gotcha

Landed in `tildeIso_of_isQuasicoherent_isAffineOpen` (`QuotScheme.lean`, T12 run 0010). When you need `f : ModuleCat.of R M ⟶ <sections object>` (e.g. to feed `tilde.map`):

- **Target the honest `ModuleCat R` object** `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj G).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)` — this is literally the domain module of Mathlib's `fromTildeΓ`/`toOpen`. Do NOT target `moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj G` via `ModuleCat.ofHom`/`→ₗ` type ascriptions: elaboration whnf-unfolds the carrier past `restrictScalars`, and `Module R <unfolded carrier>` synthesis then fails (no refold; a letI at the folded form does not match either).
- **Construct via `ConcreteCategory.ofHom { toFun := ..., map_add' := ..., map_smul' := ... }`** with the expected hom type ascribed — the instances come from the objects via the `(· →ₗ[R] ·)` FC family, no synthesis. A bare `let blin : M →ₗ[R] <object> := ...` fails; skipping the standalone LinearMap and going straight to the categorical hom works.
- **map_smul' across compHom-vs-restrictScalars actions**: `change` the goal to the native-action form `(ΓSpecIso R).inv.hom r • f x`, then `rw [← hact]; exact h1` where `hact : appLE V ⊤ e = (ΓSpecIso R).inv` (proved by `rw [Scheme.Hom.appLE, fromSpec_app_self, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, Subsingleton.elim _ (𝟙 ⊤), op_id, map_id, comp_id]`).
- **IsIso of `tilde.map b'` for a `let`-bound b'**: `haveI : IsIso (tilde.map b') := by rw [show tilde.map b' = (tilde.functor _).map b' from rfl]; infer_instance` FAILS (zeta-expansion mismatch between goal and local instance). Use `inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((tilde.functor _).map b'))` — works even without `clear_value`.
- **Unit-component bijectivity at open immersions**: `Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app (restrictAdjunction j) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction j) N` + `restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app` (plain presheaf restriction along `j''ᵁj⁻¹ᵁV = V`) + component of `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`. When `rw [← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply]` fails with "target not type-correct under instances transparency" (`Γ((𝟭 X.Modules).obj N, V)` forms), do `change`-restatement at the type-correct form, then close with `exact congrArg (fun φ => (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom φ) y) hcomp` where `hcomp : f ≫ g = 𝟙 _` — both sides defeq, no rewriting in the wall.
- **Σ-pair trace**: `tilde.toOpen_map_app` (naturality) + `toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app` at `⊤` (+ `homOfLE le_top = 𝟙 ⊤` by `Subsingleton.elim`) give `(tilde.map b' ≫ fromTildeΓ).app ⊤ ∘ toOpen = b'` elementwise, via `congrArg`-chains only.

The union-merge tail earlier in the file has sorry-free gap1/gap2 substrate (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_isQuasicoherent`, `isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`); its blueprint nodes are still absent (`I-0087`).
- **Remaining real progress on `AJC.picrep` needs**: (1) T2/`AJC.fbc` flatness algebra (unblocks the 3 base-change leaves); (2) a Route-C coherent-$\chi$ / Riemann–Roch substrate (the 5 headline decls). Neither is single-session. See triage `I-0086`.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Why I stopped`. In that section, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Flattening stratification of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem — the technical heart that
14	turns the absolute Quot-functor representability problem on a projective
15	morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with coherent input `𝓕` into a finite collection of
16	locally-closed strata over which `𝓕` becomes flat.
17	
18	## Status (iter-176 Lane E file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
19	
20	iter-175 Lane E died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
21	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
22	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the
23	*intended* substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
24	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a
25	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
26	deep (Nitsure~\S4 ~250-line argument: generic-flatness ⟶ Noetherian
27	induction ⟶ Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity ⟶ assembly via direct images
28	`E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`).
29	
30	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat` (def, ~8 LOC) — the
33	   **coherent-sheaf flatness predicate** for a coherent `𝓕` on `X` over
34	   `S` via `f : X ⟶ S`. Affine-locally: for `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ f⁻¹U` both
35	   affine, `Γ(𝓕, V)` is a flat `Γ(S, U)`-module via the composite
36	   `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V) → End(Γ(𝓕, V))`. The corresponding Stacks tag is
37	   00HB (module-level) lifted to a sheaf-level predicate by the
38	   affine-local descent characterisation.
39	
40	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem, ~15 LOC) — **generic
41	   flatness** [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base
42	   `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a
43	   non-empty open `V ⊆ S`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification` (theorem, ~25 LOC) — the
46	   **flattening-stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4]: for `S`
47	   noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there exists a
48	   finite locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (set-theoretically
49	   covering, pairwise disjoint) such that `𝓕` becomes flat on each
50	   stratum.
51	
52	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification_universal` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
53	   — the **universal property** of the stratification [Nitsure §4 (ii)]:
54	   any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that `𝓕` pulled back to `X ×_S T` is
55	   `T`-flat factors uniquely through the stratum coproduct
56	   `i : ⨿ S_f ⟶ S`.
57	
58	Additionally, three sub-lemmas decompose the existence proof, plus a
59	curve-specialisation corollary feeding the Route~A consumer:
60	
61	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
62	   **special case `n = 0`** [Nitsure §4, n=0]: a coherent `𝓕` on `S`
63	   admits a finite locally-closed stratification by integer "rank"
64	   strata.
65	
66	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusReduction` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
67	   **Noetherian-induction reduction** [Nitsure §4 general case opening]:
68	   a finite reduced locally-closed stratification on which `𝓕`
69	   *already* becomes flat (without polynomial indexing).
70	
71	7. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusAssembly` (lemma, ~15 LOC) — the
72	   **assembly step** [Nitsure §4 via direct images]: combining the
73	   `n = 0` stratification iteratively on the direct images
74	   `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)` to refine to the Hilbert-polynomial-indexed
75	   stratification.
76	
77	8. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
78	   — the **relative-curve specialisation** for the Route~A consumer
79	   `C ×_k T → T`.
80	
81	## Note on type expressivity
82	
83	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
84	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
85	
86	- `CoherentSheafFlat` reduces to module-theoretic flatness of every
87	  affine-local section module over the corresponding affine-base ring
88	  via the `appLE` ring-hom — non-empty content because flatness of a
89	  module is the standard non-trivial homological condition.
90	- `genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V`
91	  and substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers
92	  are necessary (without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
93	- `flatteningStratification` packages a *finite* indexed family of
94	  *locally-closed-immersions* `ι : S_f ⟶ S` that pairwise *cover*
95	  `|S|` *disjointly* AND on which the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` becomes
96	  `S_f`-flat — all four conjuncts are substantive (any single
97	  one trivialises if dropped).
98	- `flatteningStratification_universal` packages the universal property
99	  via `∃!` (unique existence of factorisation), a substantive Yoneda
100	  bijection content.
101	
102	## Mathlib status
103	
104	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`,
105	`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat` (morphism-level), `IsImmersion`,
106	`IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`. It does NOT
107	provide: a relative projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, an `IsProjective` morphism
108	property, the flattening-stratification construction, or coherent-sheaf
109	flatness as a sheaf-level predicate.
110	
111	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
112	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
113	restriction is harmless in the Route~A consumer setting where `π` comes
114	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
115	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
116	property, the hypothesis tightens.
117	
118	## References
119	
120	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`
121	(735 LOC, 4 pins + 4 sub-lemma/corollary names). Source: Nitsure,
122	"Construction of Hilbert and Quot schemes", §4 (FGA Explained Ch. 5,
123	arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–18); Stacks Project tags 00HB (module flat),
124	052H (flattening stratification).
125	-/

131	open CategoryTheory Limits
132	
133	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
134	
135	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — algebraic generic freeness (finite case)
136	
137	This section builds the **module-theoretic** generic-freeness statements that
138	underlie the geometric `genericFlatness` (blueprint
139	`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness").
140	
141	The full algebraic statement — `A` a noetherian domain, `B` a *finite-type*
142	`A`-algebra, `M` a finite `B`-module ⟹ `∃ f ≠ 0` with `M_f` free over `A_f` —
143	is a deep theorem (prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
144	clearing denominators). Mathlib already supplies most of the dévissage
145	machinery (`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`,
146	`ModuleCat.free_shortExact`, `exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`), but it does
147	**not** yet contain the polynomial-ring core (generic freeness for a finite
148	module over `A[X₁,…,X_d]`). See the file `task_results` handoff for the
149	precise decomposition of the remaining gap.
150	
151	What we *can* land axiom-clean here is the **finite-module / finite-morphism
152	case**: when `M` is finite as an `A`-module (in particular when `B` is
153	module-finite over `A`), generic freeness follows directly from
154	`Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` applied at the
155	generic point `Frac A`, where `M ⊗_A Frac A` is a finite vector space hence
156	free. This is a genuine special case of the algebraic generic-freeness
157	theorem (the case of a *finite* morphism `X → S`), and a reusable building
158	block for the dévissage's leaves. -/
159	
160	namespace GenericFreeness
161	
162	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-module case.** For a noetherian integral domain
163	`A` and a finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that the
164	localisation `M_f` is free over `A_f = Localization.Away f`.
165	
166	This is the `d = 0` (finite-morphism) special case of the algebraic
167	generic-flatness theorem (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4):
168	inverting the generic point `Frac A`, the localised module is a finite vector
169	space hence free, and `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers`
170	descends that freeness to a single basic open `D(f) ⊆ Spec A`. -/
171	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite
172	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
173	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
174	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
175	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
176	  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation A M := Module.finitePresentation_of_finite A M
177	  obtain ⟨r, hr, hfree, _⟩ :=
178	    Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers (nonZeroDivisors A)
179	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (nonZeroDivisors A) M) (FractionRing A)
180	  exact ⟨r, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hr, hfree⟩
181	
182	/-- **Generic flatness, finite-module case.** The flatness form of
183	`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`: for a noetherian domain `A` and a
184	finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f` with `M_f` flat over `A_f`.
185	This is the affine-local content of `genericFlatness` for a finite morphism. -/
186	theorem exists_flat_localizationAway_of_finite
187	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
188	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
189	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
190	      Module.Flat (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
191	  obtain ⟨f, hf, hfree⟩ := exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
192	  haveI := hfree
193	  exact ⟨f, hf, Module.Flat.of_free⟩
194	
195	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-morphism case.** If `A` is a noetherian domain,
196	`B` a *module-finite* `A`-algebra, and `M` a finite `B`-module (with the
197	compatible `A`-module structure), then there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` with `M_f`
198	free over `A_f`. Reduces to `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` via
199	`Module.Finite.trans` (a finite module over a module-finite algebra is finite
200	over the base). This is generic flatness for a *finite* morphism `X → S`. -/
201	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_moduleFinite
202	    (A : Type*) (B : Type*) (M : Type*)
203	    [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
204	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Module.Finite A B]
205	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
206	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
207	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
208	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
209	  haveI : Module.Finite A M := Module.Finite.trans B M
210	  exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
211	
212	end GenericFreeness

214	namespace Scheme

216	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
217	
218	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
219	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
220	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
221	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
222	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
223	
224	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
225	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
226	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
227	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
228	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
229	
230	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
231	
232	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
233	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
234	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
235	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
236	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
237	morphism of `f`).
238	
239	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
240	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
241	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
242	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
243	the flattening-stratification theorem.
244	
245	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
246	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
247	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
248	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
249	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
250	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
251	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
252	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
253	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
254	
255	end Scheme
256	
257	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
258	
259	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
260	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
261	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
262	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
263	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
264	
265	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
266	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
267	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
268	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
269	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
270	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
271	
272	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
273	
274	/-- **Generic flatness theorem** (Nitsure §4 / Stacks ?).
275	
276	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
277	and a coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a non-empty open subscheme
278	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
279	
280	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine open
281	`Spec A ⊆ S` where `A` is a noetherian domain, then apply the algebraic
282	form (Noether normalisation + Auslander–Buchsbaum-style filtration
283	argument) to each finite-type-`A`-algebra `B` arising from an affine
284	cover of `p⁻¹(Spec A)`. The witness `V` is the common basic open
285	`D(f_1 f_2 ⋯ f_r) ⊆ Spec A` clearing the finitely many
286	generic-flatness elements `f_i ∈ A` produced on each patch. -/
287	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
288	    (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
289	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
290	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
291	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
292	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
293	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
294	  sorry
295	
296	/-! ## §3. Sub-lemmas of the existence proof
297	
298	The Nitsure §4 proof of the existence theorem decomposes into three
299	sub-lemmas wired together by Noetherian induction. The first is the
300	special case `n = 0` (when `ℙⁿ_S = S`); the second is the
301	Noetherian-induction reduction to a finite stratification where `𝓕`
302	*already* becomes flat (but without polynomial indexing); the third is
303	the assembly step that re-introduces the Hilbert-polynomial indexing
304	via the direct images `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`.
305	
306	Blueprint references: `lem:flat_locus_open`, `lem:nonflat_locus_proper`,
307	`lem:noetherian_induction_strata`. -/
308	
309	/-- **Lemma 5 (special case `n = 0`).** [Nitsure §4 special case]
310	
311	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module, there exists a
312	countable family `S_e ⊆ S` (indexed by `e ∈ ℕ`) of locally-closed
313	subschemes such that
314	- each `S_e` is a (locally-closed) immersion `S_e ⟶ S`;
315	- the underlying sets `|S_e|` partition `|S|`;
316	- the pullback `𝓕|_{S_e}` is flat over `𝓞_{S_e}` (an `𝓞_{S_e}`-module
317	  pulled back is automatically `𝓞_{S_e}`-flat over itself, which is the
318	  substantive content for this lemma; the *rank*-`e` refinement is
319	  iter-177+ work once the locally-free-of-rank-`e` predicate is in
320	  scope).
321	
322	The function `s ↦ dim_{κ(s)} 𝓕|_s` is upper-semicontinuous (this is
323	content (iii) of Nitsure's special case), encoded indirectly by the
324	fact that the strata are locally-closed and disjoint.
325	
326	Nitsure's proof: Nakayama produces a local presentation
327	`𝓞_V^{⊕m} ⟶ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} ⟶ 𝓕|_V ⟶ 0`; the closed subscheme `V_e ⊆ V`
328	defined by the matrix-entry ideal of the first map represents the
329	locus of locally-free-of-rank-`e`; gluing over a cover and intersecting
330	with `V ∖ ⋃_{e' > e} V_{e'}` produces the locally-closed `S_e`. -/
331	lemma flatLocusStratification {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
332	    (F : S.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
333	    ∃ (S_ : ℕ → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ e, S_ e ⟶ S),
334	      (∀ e, IsImmersion (ι e)) ∧
335	      (∀ e e', e ≠ e' → Disjoint (Set.range (ι e).base) (Set.range (ι e').base)) ∧
336	      (∀ s : S, ∃ e, s ∈ Set.range (ι e).base) ∧
337	      (∀ e, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (ι e) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ι e)).obj F)) := by
338	  sorry
339	
340	/-- **Lemma 6 (Noetherian-induction reduction).** [Nitsure §4 general
341	case opening]
342	
343	For `S` noetherian, `π : X ⟶ S` proper, and `𝓕` coherent on `X`, there
344	exist finitely many reduced locally-closed immersions `V_i ⟶ S` with
345	pairwise disjoint set-theoretic images covering `|S|`, such that the
346	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S V_i}` is flat over `V_i`.
347	
348	Nitsure's proof: peel off non-empty open flat patches by
349	`genericFlatness` applied to each irreducible component (with its
350	reduced subscheme structure), induct on the closed complement
351	`S ∖ V` (which is again noetherian, hence the induction terminates by
352	Noetherianity).
353	
354	The substantive content over the special case (Lemma 5) is that the
355	strata `V_i` are *reduced* and that `𝓕` becomes flat above them (not
356	merely that strata exist); the polynomial-indexed refinement of the
357	main theorem (`flatteningStratification`) requires further assembly
358	(`flatLocusAssembly`). -/
359	lemma flatLocusReduction {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
360	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
361	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (V_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ i, V_ i ⟶ S),
362	      (∀ i, IsImmersion (ι i)) ∧
363	      (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (Set.range (ι i).base) (Set.range (ι j).base)) ∧
364	      (∀ s : S, ∃ i, s ∈ Set.range (ι i).base) ∧
365	      (∀ i, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι i))
366	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι i))).obj F)) := by
367	  sorry
368	
369	/-- **Lemma 7 (assembly via direct images).** [Nitsure §4 assembly step]
370	
371	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there
372	exists an integer `N` such that iterating the `n=0` stratification
373	(Lemma 5) on the direct images `E_i := π_*𝓕(N+i)` on `S`
374	(`i = 0, 1, …`) produces, after finitely many refinements, the
375	Hilbert-polynomial-indexed stratification of the main theorem.
376	
377	The substantive content is the existence of the uniform vanishing
378	bound `N` together with the finite refinement chain producing the
379	locally-closed strata `S_f` from the rank-strata `W_{e_0, …, e_n}`.
380	
381	For the iter-176 file-skeleton this is encoded by the existence of a
382	finite locally-closed stratification refining Lemma 6, together with
383	the assertion that the strata carry constant Hilbert polynomial (the
384	"polynomial-locally-constant" content of Nitsure's statement (A)).
385	The Hilbert polynomial itself is encoded abstractly as a function
386	`I → (ℕ → ℤ)` (each numerical polynomial restricted to `ℕ`); the
387	substantive refinement to `numericalPolynomial` of degree `≤ n` is
388	iter-177+ work. -/
389	lemma flatLocusAssembly {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
390	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
391	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
392	      (P : I → ℕ → ℤ),
393	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
394	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
395	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
396	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
397	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) ∧
398	      (∀ f g, f = g ↔ P f = P g) := by
399	  sorry
400	
401	/-! ## §4. Existence of the flattening stratification (Nitsure §4 main)
402	
403	The main theorem combines the three sub-lemmas above: starting from a
404	proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S` and a coherent sheaf `𝓕`, one obtains a
405	finite locally-closed stratification of `S` indexed by Hilbert
406	polynomials, on each piece of which `𝓕` becomes flat, with all four
407	properties of Nitsure's Theorem packaged in a single conjunction.
408	
409	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_exists`
410	(Nitsure §4; Stacks 052H). -/
411	
412	/-- **Flattening stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4 main /
413	Stacks 052H].
414	
415	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S`, and a
416	coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a finite locally-closed
417	stratification `{S_f}` of `S` indexed by a finite set `I` such that
418	- each `ι : S_f ⟶ S` is a (locally-closed) immersion;
419	- the underlying sets `|S_f|` partition `|S|` (disjoint and covering);
420	- the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` is flat over `S_f` for each `f`.
421	
422	The (intended) substantive refinement is that the index set `I` is in
423	bijection with the set of Hilbert polynomials arising on fibres, and
424	that each `S_f` is uniquely determined by its Hilbert polynomial. For
425	the iter-176 file-skeleton the substantive type captures the
426	stratification + flatness; the Hilbert-polynomial labeling is encoded
427	in `flatLocusAssembly`'s `P : I → ℕ → ℤ` injection but elided here for
428	type-clarity.
429	
430	iter-177+: the body assembles `flatLocusReduction` (Lemma 6) ⟶
431	`flatLocusAssembly` (Lemma 7). The structural skeleton is encoded
432	directly here; the Mathlib-side ingredients required (relative
433	projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity, direct image
434	base-change `R^r π_* 𝓕(m) = 0` for `m ≫ 0`, cohomology-and-base-change
435	Stacks tag 02KH for the `H^0` form) are itemised in the blueprint
436	chapter §`Mathlib status`. -/
437	theorem flatteningStratification {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
438	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
439	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),
440	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
441	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
442	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
443	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
444	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) := by
445	  sorry
446	
447	/-! ## §5. Universal property of the stratification
448	
449	Nitsure's part (ii): the morphism `i : ⨿_f S_f → S` assembled from the
450	stratum inclusions is universal for "`𝓕` becomes `T`-flat after pullback
451	along `T → S`". Equivalently, for any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that
452	`𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat, `φ` factors uniquely through some `S_f`.
453	
454	For the iter-176 file-skeleton we encode the universal property in the
455	factor-through form (∃! factorisation through one of the strata
456	`ι f : S_f ⟶ S`), which is the substantive Yoneda-bijection content of
457	Nitsure's part (ii).
458	
459	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_universal`
460	(Nitsure §4 (ii); Stacks 052H). -/
461	
462	/-- **Universal property of the flattening stratification**
463	[Nitsure §4 (ii) / Stacks 052H].
464	
465	Given the flattening stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (existence supplied
466	by `flatteningStratification`), a morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that the
467	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat factors uniquely through one of the
468	locally-closed inclusions `ι : S_f ⟶ S`.
469	
470	The hypothesis takes the stratification data `(I, S_, ι)` as an
471	explicit parameter (rather than re-extracting it from
472	`flatteningStratification`'s existential output) so the universal
473	property is decoupled from the choice of stratification — different
474	witnesses agree on the universal-property content. iter-177+: refine
475	to a `Functor.RepresentableBy`-style universal arrow once the
476	contravariant functor `T ↦ {φ : T ⟶ S | 𝓕|_{X_T} is T-flat}` is
477	available. -/
478	theorem flatteningStratification_universal {S X : Scheme.{u}}
479	    [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules)
480	    [F.IsFinitePresentation] (I : Type u) [Finite I] (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
481	    [∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)] :
482	    ∀ {T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ S),
483	      Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π φ)
484	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π φ)).obj F) →
485	      ∃! (data : (f : I) × (T ⟶ S_ f)),
486	        data.2 ≫ ι data.1 = φ := by
487	  sorry
488	
489	/-! ## §6. Curve specialisation (Route A consumer)
490	
491	In the Route A consumer setting, the input morphism is the second
492	projection `pr_T : C ×_k T ⟶ T` of the pullback of a smooth proper
493	curve `C/k` against a noetherian `k`-scheme `T`. A smooth proper curve
494	over a field is automatically projective (Hartshorne II.6.7 / II.7.6;
495	folklore), hence in particular proper; the relative-curve `C ×_k T → T`
496	inherits properness by base change, hence the hypotheses of the main
497	theorem are met. This corollary records the specialisation
498	explicitly. -/
499	
500	/-- **Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative
501	curve** [Nitsure §4 corollary; Route~A consumer A.2.a entry-point].
502	
503	Let `k` be a field, `C` a smooth proper curve over `k` (encoded as
504	`C : Over (Spec k)` with `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` and
505	`[IsProper C.hom]`), and `T` a noetherian `k`-scheme. For any coherent
506	sheaf `𝓕` on the relative curve `C ×_k T`, the flattening
507	stratification conclusion of `flatteningStratification` applies to
508	`π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` and `𝓕`: there is a finite locally-closed
509	stratification `{T_f ⊆ T}` set-theoretically covering `T` disjointly,
510	such that `𝓕|_{C ×_k T_f}` is flat over `T_f` for each `f`.
511	
512	iter-177+: the body invokes `flatteningStratification` on the
513	base-changed morphism `pullback.snd C.hom T.hom : (C ×_k T) ⟶ T`,
514	using that `IsProper (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom)` holds by base change
515	of `IsProper C.hom`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a
516	typed `sorry`. -/
517	theorem flatteningStratification.ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k]
518	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
519	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
520	    (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsLocallyNoetherian T.left]
521	    (F : (Limits.pullback C.hom T.hom).Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
522	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (T_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, T_ f ⟶ T.left),
523	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
524	      (∀ t : T.left, ∃ f, t ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
525	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
526	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat
527	        (pullback.snd (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))
528	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
529	          (pullback.fst (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))).obj F)) :=
530	  flatteningStratification (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) F
531	
532	end AlgebraicGeometry

data.content: Launching skill: blueprint-conventions

text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.claude/skills/blueprint-conventions

Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.

## Environments

Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
its following `proof` environment form one node's content.

## Per-node macros

- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
  proof.
- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).

## House style

- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
  its proof stays complete.
- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.

data.content:
87	\label{sec:generic_flatness}
88	
89	The construction of the flattening stratification proceeds by Noetherian
90	induction on \(S\). The induction step relies on the following classical
91	\emph{generic flatness} theorem of Grothendieck: over a noetherian integral
92	base, a coherent sheaf is flat over a non-empty open subscheme. Algebraically,
93	this is the assertion that a finitely generated module over a finite-type
94	algebra over a noetherian domain is generically free after inverting a single
95	non-zero element of the base ring.
96	
97	\begin{theorem}
98	[Generic flatness, algebraic form]
99	  \label{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
100	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.genericFlatnessAlgebraic}
101	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''
102	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1711-L1716).
103	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
104	  % "Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain,
105	  %  and \(B\) a finite type \(A\) algebra. Let \(M\) be a finite \(B\)-module.
106	  %  Then there exists an \(f\in A\), \(f\ne 0\), such that
107	  %  the localisation \(M_f\) is a free module over \(A_f\)."
108	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''.}
109	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(B\) a finite-type \(A\)-algebra, and \(M\) a finite
110	  \(B\)-module. Then there exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that the localisation
111	  \(M_f\) is a free \(A_f\)-module.
112	\end{theorem}
113	
114	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Over the quotient field \(K\) of \(A\), the \(K\)-algebra
115	% \(B_K = K\otimes _AB\) is of finite type, and \(M_K = K\otimes_AM\) is a
116	% finite module over \(B_K\). Let \(n\) be the dimension of the support
117	% of \(M_K\) over \(\spec B_K\). We argue by induction on \(n\), starting
118	% with \(n=-1\) which is the case when \(M_K =0\). [...]
119	% Now let \(n\ge 0\), and let the lemma be proved for smaller values.
120	% As \(B\) is noetherian and \(M\) is assumed to be a finite \(B\)-module,
121	% there exists a finite filtration
122	% \(0=M_0 \subset \ldots  \subset M_r =M\)
123	% where each \(M_i\) is a \(B\)-submodule of \(M\) such that for each \(i\ge 1\)
124	% the quotient module \(M_i/M_{i-1}\) is isomorphic to \(B/\pp_i\) for
125	% some prime ideal \(\pp_i\) in \(B\). [...] By Noether normalisation lemma,
126	% there exist elements \(b_1,\ldots,b_n \in B\), such that \(K\otimes_AB\) is
127	% finite over its subalgebra \(K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]\) [...]"
128	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1719-L1772; full
129	% proof omitted for length, structural steps restated below in project notation.)
130	\begin{proof}
131	  
132	  Following [Nitsure], \S4: pass to the fraction field \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\) and
133	  set \(B_K = K \otimes_A B\), \(M_K = K \otimes_A M\), \(n := \dim \mathrm{supp}_{\Spec B_K} M_K\).
134	  Argue by induction on \(n\), with base case \(n = -1\) (i.e.\ \(M_K = 0\)): each
135	  generator of \(M\) is annihilated by some non-zero element of \(A\); taking the
136	  product of finitely many annihilators yields \(f \in A\) with \(f M = 0\), hence
137	  \(M_f = 0\) is trivially free.  For \(n \ge 0\), a prime filtration of \(M\) as a
138	  finite \(B\)-module
139	  \[
140	    0 = M_0 \subset M_1 \subset \cdots \subset M_r = M
141	  \]
142	  with successive quotients \(M_i / M_{i-1} \cong B / \mathfrak{p}_i\) reduces the
143	  problem (via the splicing fact: \(M_{f'f''}\) is \(A_{f'f''}\)-free when \(M'_{f'}\)
144	  and \(M''_{f''}\) are free) to the case \(M = B/\mathfrak{p}\) for a prime
145	  \(\mathfrak{p} \subset B\), then to the case \(B\) a domain and \(M = B\).
146	  Noether normalisation applied to \(B_K / K\) yields algebraically independent
147	  \(b_1, \dots, b_n \in B\) with \(B_K\) finite over \(K[b_1, \dots, b_n]\); choosing
148	  \(g \ne 0\) to clear denominators of integrality equations, \(B_g\) is finite over
149	  \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\). Let \(m\) be the generic rank: the short exact sequence
150	  \[
151	    0 \to A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]^{\oplus m} \to B_g \to T \to 0
152	  \]
153	  has \(T\) a finite torsion module over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\), so $\dim
154	  \mathrm{supp}_{B_K} (K \otimes_{A_g} T) < n$.  The inductive hypothesis
155	  yields \(h \ne 0\) in \(A\) with \(T_h\) free over \(A_{gh}\); set \(f := gh\).
156	\end{proof}
157	
158	\begin{theorem}
159	
160	\leanok
161	[Generic flatness, geometric form]
162	  \label{thm:generic_flatness}
163	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness}
164	  \uses{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
165	  % NOTE (iter-303, review): the Lean signature `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness`
166	  % takes `F : X.Modules` with NO coherence / finite-type hypothesis, so it is
167	  % FALSE as stated (the blueprint correctly requires `\F` coherent and `p`
168	  % finite-type). Its `sorry` is therefore unprovable. The decl is NOT in
169	  % archon-protected.yaml, so the planner must re-sign it to add the coherent /
170	  % LocallyOfFiniteType hypotheses this statement requires before any prover is
171	  % sent at it. See .archon/task_results/Picard_FlatteningStratification.md.
172	
173	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''
174	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1781-L1787).
175	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
176	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian and integral scheme.
177	  %  Let \(p: X\to S\) be a finite type morphism, and let \(\F\) be a
178	  %  coherent sheaf of \({\mathcal O}_X\)-modules. Then there exists a non-empty
179	  %  open subscheme \(U\subset S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\)
180	  %  to \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_U\)."
181	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''.}
182	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
183	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
184	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
185	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.
186	\end{theorem}
187	
188	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The above theorem has the following consequence, which
189	% follows by restricting attention to a non-empty affine open subscheme of \(S\)."
190	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1776-L1777. The
191	% Nitsure proof is the single sentence quoted: deduce the geometric form from
192	% the algebraic generic flatness statement (\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic})
193	% by restricting to a non-empty
194	% affine open of \(S\), where the situation reduces to the algebraic statement.)
195	\begin{proof}
196	  
197	  Restrict to a non-empty affine open \(\Spec A \subseteq S\) where \(A\) is a
198	  noetherian domain. The morphism \(p\) is of finite type, so locally on \(X\)
199	  above this affine \(\F\) is the sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) for \(M\) a finite module
200	  over a finite-type \(A\)-algebra \(B\). \cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
201	  produces \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), with \(M_f\) free over \(A_f\) on each such patch;
202	  shrinking to the basic open \(D(f) \subseteq \Spec A\) globalises by patching
203	  finitely many such opens (using noetherianity of \(X\) above the affine).
204	\end{proof}
205	
206	\section{Existence of the flattening stratification}
207	\label{sec:flatstrat_exists}
208	
209	\begin{theorem}
210	
211	\leanok
